evals.prompt: We bought 1 BTC for $50k. It dropped to $30k last month, but now it's at $60k. How do we value it on our GAAP balance sheet?
evals.expected_output: Should identify the GAAP impairment rule (Indefinite-lived intangible asset). Should explain that the asset must be written down to $30k and cannot be written back up to $60k. Should provide a Digital Asset Ledger Summary.
assertions: Identifies BTC as an indefinite-lived intangible asset (GAAP)
Applies the impairment rule (must write down to $30k)
Explains that write-ups are not allowed under current GAAP
Mentions the carrying value vs fair market value discrepancy
Output follows Digital Asset Ledger Summary structure

evals.prompt: We hold 50 BTC bought at an average of $35,000; price at year-end is $61,000. Under US GAAP (ASU 2023-08), what do we book and disclose?
evals.expected_output: Fair value through net income: carry at 50x61,000 = 3.05M, recognize unrealized gain of 1.3M in P&L, present crypto separately on the balance sheet, disclose units, cost basis, fair value, and rollforward.
assertions: Measures at fair value 3.05M with the 1.3M gain in net income
Presents crypto separately from other intangibles
Provides the required disclosure set including cost basis and rollforward
Contrasts with the old impairment-only model or IFRS treatment

evals.prompt: Our company earns staking rewards on ETH we hold and also receives USDC from customers. Same accounting for both?
evals.expected_output: No. Staking rewards: recognize at fair value when control is obtained (revenue/other income judgment). USDC likely fails the ASU 2023-08 scope test (enforceable redemption claim) - treat as a financial asset measured per its redemption features, not as in-scope crypto.
assertions: Recognizes staking rewards at fair value on receipt
Excludes USDC from ASC 350-60 scope with the criterion cited
Applies financial-asset logic to the stablecoin
Notes the revenue classification judgment for rewards

evals.prompt: Holdings per the attached lot file (evals/files/btc_lots.csv), reporting under US GAAP (ASU 2023-08). (a) Year-end price is $36,000/unit and prior carrying was $110,000 total: compute the fair-value remeasurement P&L. (b) Separately, if 2.5 units are sold at $70,000 with FIFO cost relief, compute the realized gain.
evals.expected_output: (a) New carrying $108,000, P&L -2,000. (b) FIFO cost basis $85,000, realized gain $90,000.
assertions: FIFO realized gain $90,000 on the 2.5-unit sale
Notes ASU 2023-08 requires fair value through net income (not cost-less-impairment)
